evidence: GEMINI.md lines 127-141: validation-state.json schema defined with assertions object mapping VAL IDs to {status} where status is one of: pending, passed, failed, blocked
GEMINI.md line 141: 'status (string) — One of: pending, passed, failed, blocked.'
GEMINI.md lines 128-137: Example schema shows assertions object with VAL-XXX-001 through VAL-XXX-004 demonstrating all four status values
user-testing-validator.md lines 145-166: Phase 3.2 'Update validation-state.json' instructs updating this file for every tested assertion with status and evidence
mission-planner SKILL.md line 467-497: Section 7.3 'Initialize validation-state.json' instructs creating the file with every assertion ID initialized to pending
Actual validation-state.json in mission dir confirms schema: top-level assertions object with VAL-XXX-NNN keys each having a status field
assertions.notes: Schema is defined in GEMINI.md Section 3.3. User-testing validator explicitly references updating this file. Planner skill defines initialization. Actual mission file matches the schema.

evidence: scrutiny-validator.md lines 64-77: Phase 1.3 'Check for Prior Synthesis' — reads existing report at .mission/validation/<milestone>/scrutiny/synthesis.json, extracts previously failed items, limits re-validation to those failures, still re-runs hard-gate commands
scrutiny-validator.md line 73: 'Extract the features, files, commands, or findings that previously failed.'
scrutiny-validator.md line 74: 'Limit re-validation to those failing areas instead of re-reviewing every successful area in full.'
scrutiny-validator.md line 77: 'If no prior report exists, perform a full scrutiny pass.'
scrutiny-validator.md lines 153-155: Phase 3.4 'Scope Re-Validation Intelligently' — concentrates review on previously failed items
scrutiny-validator.md line 262: Important Rule 4: 'Always check the previous synthesis report first when it exists, and only re-validate failed areas'

user-testing-validator.md lines 70-83: Phase 1.3 'Check for Prior Synthesis' — reads existing report at .mission/validation/<milestone>/user-testing/synthesis.json, extracts failed/blocked assertions, re-tests only those
user-testing-validator.md line 79: 'Extract assertions previously marked failed or blocked.'
user-testing-validator.md line 81: 'Do not spend time re-testing assertions that already passed and are unchanged.'
user-testing-validator.md line 83: 'If no prior report exists, perform a full test pass for all in-scope assertions.'
user-testing-validator.md line 285: Important Rule 6: 'Always check the previous synthesis report first and only re-test failures or blocked assertions'
assertions.notes: Both validators have explicit Phase 1.3 sections for checking prior synthesis reports and scoping re-validation to previously failed/blocked items. This is reinforced in their Important Rules sections.
